warnings.message: Some PDF pages have no strong text-window match in the EPUB
Some dense PDF word windows are missing from the EPUB and may indicate sliced sentence interiors
Some early CONTENTS pages have no strong text-window match in the EPUB
Some first body lines near the top of PDF pages are not found in the EPUB
Some last body lines near the bottom of PDF pages are not found in the EPUB
Some adjacent EPUB paragraphs look like possible faulty line or page breaks
Some paragraphs begin with large bare numbers that may be broken reference continuations
Some roman numeral headings appear in body paragraphs instead of centered heading elements
Some chapter headings are long enough to suggest swallowed body text
Some EPUB enumerators look like possible sequence jumps and need triage
Repeated word windows may indicate ghost-layer duplication
A significant portion of Latin words in the EPUB are not wrapped in language spans
Some tagged Latin phrases in the EPUB do not have matching modern translations in translation_db.py

short_fragments.text: Come we now to the uses.
WE shall now proceed to the uses.
Use all diligence in this matter.
One word of use, and I have done.
There are divers reasons of it: —
One word of use, and I have done.
The Lord prosper it in your hands!
This is the preface of the song.
I would speak unto three things: —
I will name a few things: —

DISCOURSE 2. 69 "The cup of blessing which we bless, is it not the communion of the blood of Christ? The bread which we break, is it not the communion of the body of Christ?" — 1 Corinthians 10:16.
DISCOURSE 3. 70 "The cup of blessing which we bless, is it not the communion of the blood of Christ? The bread which we break, is it not the communion of the body of Christ?" — 1 Corinthians 10:16.
DISCOURSE 8. 76 "Christ also hath once suffered for sins, the just for the unjust, that he might bring us to God; being put to death in the flesh, but quickened by the Spirit." — 1 Peter 3:18.
DISCOURSE 10. 78 "Teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I have commanded you: and, lo, I am with you alway, even unto the end of the world." — Matthew 28:20.

DISCOURSE 19. 86 "I am crucified with Christ: nevertheless I live; yet not I, but Christ liveth in me: and the life which I now live in the flesh I live by the faith of the Son of God, who loved me, and gave himself for me." — Galatians 2:20.
